name: Villa of M. Cincius Hilarianus (Nador)
description: Often inaccurately called "the castellum of Nador", the monumental stone facade and entry of this late Roman farmstead still stands on the south side of Algerian Route nationale 11 in the municipal territory of Nador. The site was extensively excavated in the late 20th century and, among other finds, yielded (in conditions of re-use) a partial milestone with Roman imperial titulature dateable to 312/313 CE.

name: Villa of Caius Olius Ampliatus
description: A 'Villa rustica' associated with one Caius Olius Ampliatus, located in the Ponticelli of Naples, Italy. The site was excavated in 1986-1987, with additional work in 2007. The site covers some 2,000 sq. meters.

name: Roman villa at Híjar en Las Gabias

name: House of N. Popidius Priscus
description: A Pompeian domus that, upon excavation, was found to have a large supply of colored marble.

name: Villa romana de Salar
description: A villa with polychrome mosaics, dating from the first to the fifth centuries. It is located at modern Salar, in the Spanish province of Granada.

name: Casale Ciuffa
description: The Casale Ciuffa occupied the site of a Roman villa; this site now lies within the footprint of the Parco degli Ulivi in the modern comune of Colonna, Lazio.

name: Case Romane del Celio
description: An archaeological area beneath the basilica of the martyrs John and Paul that has been explored by means of excavation, revealing a series of Imperial period domestic remains dating between the second and fourth centuries A.D.

name: House of Augustus
description: An elite domus located at the southwest corner of the Palatine Hill that is identified as the house lived in by the Roman emperor Augustus.

name: Münster-Sarmsheim Roman Villa
description: Remains of a Roman villa, including mosaic pavements, were discovered.

name: Villa Romana di Boscorotto
description: A Roman villa with an area of more than 1,000 square meters was first identified at In Boscorotto, between the Maddaloni and Acerra districts, in 1987. Excavations were carried out between 1992 and 1993 during the construction of the Marcianise-Maddaloni freight yard.

name: Zou
description: At Zou are the remains of a palatial building of the Minoan period, built during MM IIIB.

name: Masseria Petrolio
description: The substruction (basis) of a Roman villa rustica dating to the late republican period located near the course of the Garigliano river.

name: Villa Prato, Sperlonga
description: A villa rustica excavated by the École française de Rome between 1979 and 1984.

name: Montelabate
description: The Montelabate estate of the Gaslini foundation has been investigated by means of both field survey and excavation. This upland area sits astride a frontier zone, one that separates Perugia (Etruria) from Gubbio (Umbria). Simon Stoddart, Maurizio Matteini Chiari, Gabriele Cifani, and other scholars have noted that this ancient frontier zone was in place by the fifth-fourth centuries BC and denotes the differential urbanism practices of Etruscans and Umbrians, respectively.

name: Vilarenc
description: The villa dates from the first century B.C. to the third century A.D. when it was transformed into a bath complex. It was partially reclaimed as a villa in late Roman and Medieval times.

name: Roman villa at Can Terrés
description: A Roman villa occupied from the first century B.C. until the fourth / fifth centuries A.D. The villa included a bath complex.

name: Puente Melchor Roman villa
description: A Roman villa with mosaic decoration located in an archaeological area of Puente Melchor, in Puerto Real, Cádiz, Spain.

name: House of Sallust
description: The House of Sallust (VI 2,4) is a domus in Pompeii that assumes its final form around the second century B.C.

name: House of the Small Fountain
description: A Samnite-type house located on the Via di Mercurio (VI, 8, 23). The house derives its conventional name from a mosaic fountain located at the rear of the peristyle.

name: Roman villa at Verige Bay
description: A Roman villa rustica located in the bay of Verige, dating from the first century B.C. through the sixth century A.D. The villa's ruins lie within Croatia's Brijuni National Park.

name: Faversham Roman villa
description: The site of a Roman corridor villa dating from the late first century to ca. 300 CE, excavated in 1965.

name: Villa Redenta
description: An ancient suburban villa outside of Spoletium, occupied from the Augustan period to the third century A.D. The remains are on the grounds of the modern Villa Redenta complex.

name: Linguella Roman villa
description: A multi-phase villa on the island of Elba.

name: Villa of L. Crassius Tertius
description: The Villa of L. Crassius Tertius at Oplontis (modern Torre Annunziata in Italy) was discovered in 1974, approximately 250m east of the Villa of Poppaea.

Its decorative program includes polychrome mosaic pavements. It is noted for mosaic scenes featuring the Good Shepherd.

name: Casa di Championnet

name: Unnamed villa

name: House of Serenos at Trimithis
description: Building B1 of Area 2.1 at Trimithis (Amheida) is a domus with an annexed stable dated to the 4th century CE.

name: Villa Romana di Collesecco
description: A villa rustica located in the Sabina, ca. 1 km from Cottanello (Rieti). The imperial villa was superimposed over a villa of the republican period.

name: Lechlade Roman villa
description: A Roman villa partially excavated in 1957 and 1959 by Margaret Jones and in 1981-2 by Tim Allen near Roughground Farm.

name: Poggio del Molino
description: A Roman villa overlooking the Tyrrhenian Sea located within the former territory of Populonia.

name: House of the Two Pools
description: According to Leveau (1982), the so-called "House of the Two Pools" is located to the south of the Rivet barracks. This was a large peristyle-type domus that included two nymphaea. The latter were published by Cumont and Stern.

name: Villa Commedia
description: A villa on Lake Como described by Pliny the Younger. Scholars have long thought it must be located in the vicinity of modern Lierna, and 19th and 20th century finds of floor mosaics and other elements have led to conjectures that the villa must have been located either in the peninsular "Castello" neighborhood or in the core of the old town, which has long been known as "Villa" because of this conjecture. A mosaic from the latter area is now conserved in the Archaeological Museum of Lecco.

name: Wesseling Roman Villa
description: A villa rustica at modern Wesseling.

name: Betitius Perpetuus Arzygius, Domus
description: A domus included in Regio III, the house of Betitius Perpetuus Arzygius was located on the Quirinal Hill close to the ara incendii Neroniani. Some of its remains have been documented beneath the present Palazzo delle Esposizioni.

name: Villa of Agrippa
description: The so-called Villa (or baths) of Agrippa on Planasia Ins. (modern Pianosa) is the putative location of exile for Agrippa Postumus, grandson of Augustus.

name: Villa di Casale Ghella
description: A villa complex at the eighth mile of the Via Cassia, to which it was linked by means of a paved diverticulum. The first phase dates to the first century BCE and the villa remained in use until the Late Antique period.

name: House of the Ara Maxima
description: The House of the Ara Maxima, located on the Via del Vesuvio, is also known as the House of Pinarius and the House of Narcissus (VI, 16, 15-17).

name: House of Trebius Valens
description: The House of Aulus Trebius Valens (III,2,1) is an atrium-style house in Pompeii that lies on the north side of Via dell'Abbondanza. It was first excavated in 1913.

name: San Pedro del Arroyo
description: A late Roman villa complex that continued in use during the Visigothic period.

name: Torreblanca del Sol (Fuengirola)
description: An imperial Roman bath site.

name: Karantinnaya sloboda
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 K2 Karantinnaya sloboda

name: Partizany
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 L2 Partizany

name: Yubileynoye 1
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 M2 Yubileynoye 1

name: Yubileynoye 2
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 L2 Yubileynoye 2

name: Unnamed villa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 L2 no. 151 (Vyshesteblievskaya 10 RUS)

name: Praedia Quadratiana
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 63 A4 Praedia Quadratiana

name: Villa Palmati
description: Jones describes this place as the site of an 'imperial stud farm' that produced race horses.

name: Bünyan
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 64 B3 Bünyan

name: Makelle
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 64 B3 Makelle

name: Shukhuti
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 H2 Shukhuti

name: Kings Weston Roman Villa
description: A Roman villa near Lawrence Weston northwest of Bristol, England.

name: Villa de Santa Catarina de Sítimos
description: A Roman villa of the imperial period, in use from the first through fifth centuries A.D.

name: La Torrecilla Roman Villa
description: Remains of a Roman villa dating to the second century CE.

name: House of the Dioscuri
description: A large and well-appointed house that takes its name from frescoes of Castor and Pollux (VI, 9, 6).

name: House of Apollo
description: Located on the Via di Mercurio, the so-called House of Apollo draws its name from a cycle of frescoes of the myth of Apollo found at the rear of the garden. One M. Herenullius Communis, whose name appears on a ring found in 1830, may have been the owner (VI, 7, 23).

name: House of the Baker
description: House in Pompeii (VI,3,3) that most likely dates from the second century BC and experienced extensive remodeling after the earthquake of AD 62.

name: Beit Farhi
description: Ottoman period house located in Damascus' Old City built by the Sephardic Jewish Farhi family begun in 1780.

name: Villa Dionysos (Knossos)
description: A Roman-period urban 'villa' at Knossos was excavated by R. W. Hutchinson (1935) and M. R. E. Gough (post-World War II). Based on pottery evidence, the villa dates to the second century CE and comes to be destroyed ca. 170-180.

name: Civita Giuliana
description: The Civita Giuliana is located in the northern suburbium of Pompeii, about 700 meters from the city walls. Thus far the site has produced archaeological evidence of suburban villas in Pompeii's hinterland. Early twentieth-century excavations were carried out here, only to be published in 1994. Clandestine looting at the site brought about a cooperative effort to resume excavations in the interests of heritage protection in 2017.

name: Sant Amanç de Viladés
description: The archaeological site of Sant Amanç de Viladés includes multi-period remains, stretching from indigenous Iberian remains to those of a Roman villa to Medieval occupation.

name: Vigna Bevilacqua
description: In 1834, Edward Dodwell documented a substantial tract of polygonal masonry walling at Bevilacqua near Frascati that was thought to be associated with a Roman villa platform.

name: Chatalka
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 C6 Chatalka

name: Horia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 F3 Horia

name: Madara
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 E5 Madara

name: Mogilets
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 D5 Mogilets

name: Prisovo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 C5 Prisovo

name: Teliţa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 F3 Teliţa

name: Alto de Martim Vaz
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 C3 Alto de Martim Vaz

name: Boides
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 F1 Boides

name: Arienzo
description: A villa of the second century AD that was destroyed by a flood in the fourth century.

name: Gallo-Roman villa at La Grande Terre, Chandon
description: A Gallo-Roman villa located in the Sornin valley, Loire, France.

name: Avezzano Roman villa
description: A large Roman villa near Alba Fucens that originates in the second century B.C.

name: Callow Hill Roman villa
description: A Roman villa measuring approximately 138 by 216 m dating to the last quarter of the first century CE.

name: Villa of Caesar and Maxentius at San Cesareo
description: The so-called Villa of Caesar and Maxentius at San Cesareo is located at the 18th mile of the Via Labicana.

props.ancientName: Villa di Cesare e Massenzio a San Cesareo

name: Casa della Biblioteca
description: The House of the Library (VI, 17, 41) at Pompeii is located along the Vicolo del Farmacista, south of its junction with the Via Consolare. The site was first excavated in 1759 and again during the 1970s.

name: House of M. Spurius Saturninus and D. Volcius Modestus
description: A domus in Pompeii that was built using Sarno limestone. The house was excavated in 1760, 1841, and 1910 and re-examined in 2007 and 2010. The site was bombed World War Two (13 September 1943).

name: Casa del Diavolo
description: A Roman villa (second to fourth centuries A.D.) located near ancient Forentum.

name: Domus Romana "delle Entrate"
description: The remains of a Roman townhouse (domus) at Castra Albana.

name: Villa rustica of Szentendre-Skanzen
description: A so-called Roman "Villa Rustica" covering some 5,200 square meters located at the Szabadtéri Néprajzi Múzeum (Skanzen), Szentendre.

name: Lower Warbank Roman Villa
description: A Romano-British villa near Keston, built over an earlier Iron Age site. Findspot of RIB 3025.

name: Morgantina, House of Ganymede
description: The House of Ganymede at Morgantina is a peristyle house with mosaic floor pavements. The earliest phase of the house belongs to the third century B.C.

name: Albinia Roman villa
description: The remains of a villa rustica near Orbetello, found to have construction in opus latericium.

name: Villa Romana ai Cavallacci
description: A Roman villa first occupied in the Late Republican period and abandoned ca. the fifth century AD.

name: Casa de la Fortuna
description: A Roman domus at Cartagena, Spain, dating to the first century BCE.

name: Kupinovik Roman Villa
description: A Roman villa site on the modern Croatian island of Hvar (ancient Pharus).

name: Villa Sora
description: A coastal villa in the 'contrada Sora' area of Torre del Greco, a district with a great many archaeological remains.

name: Unnamed Place
description: An unnamed place of Tripolitana

name: Barcola
description: A no-longer-visible villa maritima was identified north of Tergeste in 1887. According to AdriAtlas, a reanalysis by Fontana (1993) places the beginnings of the villa's occupational sequence in the third quarter of the first century BCE.

name: Untitled
description: Unlabeled feature captured from the Barrington Atlas

name: Can Cortada Roman villa
description: A Roman villa located in the vicinity of the Can Cortada farmhouse in the Horta-Guinardó district of Barcelona that was discovered in July 1987. It was dated to the second century BCE.

name: Unnamed Roman villa
description: Ruins of an unnamed Roman villa located near Ladispoli and in the vicinity of the so-called "Villa di Pompeo".

name: Villa dell'Incastro
description: An ancient villa in Guidonia Montecelio, Lazio.

name: Villa di Tor Vergata, località Carcaricola
description: An important villa that was excavated during the construction of the University of Rome 'Tor Vergata' in the 1970s, now reinterred. The Carcaricola villa begins in the middle of the second century B.C. and continues in use until the second century A.D.

name: House of the masks, Hadrumetum
description: An elite house in Hadrumetum known especially for its program of polychromatic mosaics dating to the third century CE.

name: Villa dei Quattro Venti
description: A monumental villa complex that is known as the “Villa dei Quattro Venti” is situated on a south-eastern offshoot of the Circeo promontory, approximately 100 meters southeast of the historic center of S. Felice Circeo.

name: Villa di Faragola
description: A Late Roman villa site near Ausculum.

name: Wehringen villa rustica
description: A series of villa rustica located along the road to Cambodunum.

name: Acquoria
description: A basis villae near Tivoli in opus incertum on the river Aniene measuring 70 x 20 m. Two phases have been identified: second-first century B.C. and first century A.D.

name: Dragoncello
description: Dragoncello is a modern district of Rome's metropolitan area where archaeological evidence for a series of late republican villas was uncovered. At least four in number, these villa rustica sites have evidence for domestic production.

name: Villa of Diomedes
description: A suburban villa of Pompeii located outside the Herculaneum gate, the Villa of Diomedes was excavated between 1771 and 1774 and named after the tomb of Marcus Arrius Diomedes, which faces the entrance on the opposite side of the road.

name: Torvaianica
description: A Roman villa near Pomezia discovered in 2006. The rich remains of the villa were uncovered along with at least 69 tombs.

name: House L7-H
description: Private house at Dura-Europos later adapted for use as synagogue phase 1.

name: Fundus Seneci(...)
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 F4 Fundus Seneci(...)

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 E4 Numituriana?/Saltus Caeliae Maximae

name: Saltus Philomusianus
description: Saltus Philomusianus was a sizable imperial estate located between Bulla Regia and Chimtou. Roman soldiers of legio III Augusta seem to have garrisoned a praesidium in the estate ca. 52-57 CE.

name: Saltus Tuzritanus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 D3 Saltus Tuzritanus

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 D4 Saltus Udensis

name: Winningen
description: A Roman villa rustica.

name: House of the Ceii
description: A Roman house in Pompeii (I, 6, 15), also known as the House of L. Ceius Secundus. It is also referred to as the House of Fabia and Tyrannus.

name: House of the Lararium of Achilles
description: The so-called "House of the Lararium of Achilles" (I ,6 ,4) lies on the Via dell'Abbondanza. Excavated in 1912 and 1913, it is also known as the "House of the Ilion Sanctuary".

name: Andernos-les-Bains
description: A Gallo-Roman villa.

name: Isera
description: A Roman villa located in the Alto-Adige region of Italy.

name: The "Auditorium site" at the Auditorium Parco della Musica (Rome)
description: An Early Republican to Middle Imperial elite complex along the Via Flaminia in the outskirts of Rome.

name: Unnamed Roman Villa
description: The site of a probable Roman villa located near Farndon, Cheshire.

name: Domus Liceo Cavour
description: An urban domus of the Umbrii located beneath what is now the Liceo scientifico Camillo Cavour in central Rome.

name: San Felice Roman villa
description: The villa site is approximately 1 km south of the presumed ancient course of the Via Appia.

name: Villa romana di Acque Fresche a Civitavecchia
description: Ruins of a Roman coastal villa located at Acque Fresche in the comune of Civitavecchia.

name: Les Convents
description: The remains of a Gallo-Roman villa at the site known as Les Convents, located on a plateau overlooking the Durance.

name: House of the Ancient Hunt
description: A well-appointed atrium house with Fourth Style wall paintings (VII,4,48).

name: Paduline villa
description: A Roman villa located on the Bruna river that was in use from the third century B.C. until the fifth century A.D.

name: Staranzano, Roman villa “Liberta Peticia”
description: A Roman villa of the mid-first century A.D.

name: Damblainville
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 7 F3 Damblainville

name: La Roche-Maurice
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 7 B3 La Roche-Maurice

name: St-Frégant
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 7 B3 St-Frégant

name: Ag. Leontios
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 A3 Ag. Leontios

name: Ammos tou Kampouri
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 E3 Ammos tou Kampouri

name: Androklou Oikos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C2 Androklou Oikos

name: Kalogiannis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 E3 Kalogiannis

name: Korakistres
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 E3 Korakistres

name: Vouni
description: Vouni is located on the northwest coast of Cyprus, some 7 km west of Soloi. The rocky hill was occupied in antiquity and the settlement included a fortified palace enclosure, the ancient name of which is unknown. According to PECS, the palace is to be dated ca. 500 to 380 BCE, with two main architectural phases (I: ca. 500 to 450 BCE, II: ca. 450 to 380 BCE).

name: Masseria Caronella
description: A Roman villa rustica located near Taranto.

name: Villa Mattonara
description: A coastal villa and associated necropolis.

name: Roman villa of Ammaia
description: The Roman villa of Ammaia dates to the first century.

name: Samnite House
description: The so-called "Samnite House" is a domus in Herculaneum that originally dates to the second century B.C., making it one of the oldest buildings discovered at the site.

name: House of the Chaste Lovers
description: The so-called House of the Chaste Lovers at Pompeii is located on the north side of the Via dell'Abbondanza. The site has been the subject of ongoing archaeological excavation since 1987. It is located in insula 12 of Regio IX. It adjoins the so-called House of the Painters at Work which lies to the north.

name: Torcularium villa
description: A Roman villa of the early second century BCE, constructed in opus incertum and so named because of the presence of a torcularium.

name: Izola
description: The remains of a Roman villa maritima.

name: Kamares
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 D4 Kamares

name: S'Imbalconadu Roman villa
description: A multi-phase rustic structure (fattoria) located near Olbia, Sardinia. The quadrangular structure consists of twenty-some rooms arranged around a central courtyard; the built surface covers ca 1,000 square meters.

name: Villa di Nerone
description: Nero built a villa and a port at Antium. The villa was subsequently expanded under Domitian and other later emperors.

name: Praedia Galbana
description: A tract of suburban land located southwest of the Aventine Hill that would come to be the location of the Horrea Galbae.

name: House of the Four Styles
description: The House of the Four Styles or Casa dei Quattro Stili (I.8.17) is noted for having examples of all four style of Pompeian wall paintings.

name: Negrar Roman Villa
description: Remains of a Roman villa with mosaic pavements located near the modern comune of Negrar di Valpolicella were first discovered in 1922.

name: Villa di Rufione
description: The Villa di Rufione is a villa rustica located along the via Flaminia Vetus, between the hamlets of Montecchio and Bastardo in località Toccioli. The site begins its life in the late republican period and continues in use until the fourth century CE.

name: Domus of Vespasia Polla
description: A Roman domus traditionally associated with Vespasia Polla, mother of the emperor Vespasian.

name: Tigellio Roman villa
description: An elite villa dated to the first century B.C.

name: Casa della Regina Carolina
description: A domus at Pompeii partially excavated in the nineteenth century.

name: Laneion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F2 Laneion

name: Via delle Piagge
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 Via delle Piagge

name: Le Vignacce
description: A Roman villa located on via Lemonia in Rome's suburbium attributed to Quintus Servilius Pudens.

name: Villa Aldobrandini
description: The Villa Aldobrandini is located on the outskirts of Frascati. The current villa of the site was built in 1550 by a Vatican prelate named Alessandro Rufini. Subsequently, Pope Clement VIII gave the villa to his nephew Cardinal Pietro Aldobrandini in 1598. The Papal villa and gardens occupy the site of a Roman terrace villa.

name: Villa di Vibio Varo
description: The so-called Villa of Vibius Varus is located south of the Villa Adriana at Tivoli. The site has three architectural phases stretching from the first century BCE to the fourth century CE

name: Villa Domitiani
description: Villa Domitiani, a large imperial villa near the Alban Lake, now the site of the Papal retreat at Castel Gandolfo.

name: Villa Esmeade
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 B2 Villa Esmeade

name: Villa Hadriani
description: The Villa of Hadrian (Villa Adriana in Italian) is a sprawling archaeological complex at Tivoli, Italy. The villa complex was designed and used by the emperor Hadrian in the second century A.D.

name: Villa Lucidi
description: A large villa complex that was built sometime in the first century B.C.E. and remained in use until at least the third century C.E.

name: Villa Muti
description: The Villa Muti in Grottaferrata occupies, at least in part, the site of a Roman republican villa built on a series of terraces and likely dating to the third and second centuries B.C. Lanciani excavated a series of rooms at the site. The site also had an early to mid-Imperial phase that included elaborate floors and decoration in imported marble.

name: Villa Neronis
description: The Villa Neronis was a rural villa of the emperor Nero, likely built early in his reign (perhaps before AD 60).

name: Villa Torlonia
description: The extant Villa Torlonia occupies the site of an ancient Roman villa, although, as Marzano notes, the interpretation of this villa and its Roman antecedents has been (and continues) to be confused in scholarship, particularly with reference to villas associated with Lucullus.

name: Villamagna
description: A large imperial Roman villa known from letters of Marcus Aurelius.

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 no. 7 (villa N Fidenae)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 no. 19 (villa near S. Sinforosa)

name: Untitled
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 no. 24 (villa, NW Pantano Secco)

name: Unnamed villa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 no. 26 (villa at Giardinetti)

name: Villa del Torraccio, Torrenova
description: The Forma Italiae notes the presence of drainage channels, foundations, and a cistern that are taken to be indicative of a Roman villa in the locale known today as Torrenova in the eastern portion of modern Rome.

name: Roman villa near Praeneste
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 no. 41 (villa near Praeneste)

name: Villas on Via Castrimeniense
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 no. 43 (villas on Via Castrimeniense)

name: Vigna Spinabella
description: A Roman villa located at Vigna Spinabella and included in the Barrington Atlas. In the Barrington Atlas directory, this place is included as a "Numbered Feature" and not given a specific title. It is assigned number 44 on BAtlas map 43 C2.

name: So-called Villa on the Lake
description: Lugli describes the remains of a sumptuous, porticoed villa in the Doric order facing onto the Alban lake. Marzano clarifies Lugli's earlier summary of the site, which was in turn based on observations made in 1912. The remains of the villa include a 200-m long portico on two levels. About 260 m from the portico are the remains of a cistern in opus reticulatum. Chiarucci documented a structure with five barrel-vaulted rooms of unclear function. Lugli's initial report suggests that this late republican villa was eventually incorporated into the Villa of Domitian.

name: Roman Villa at Lago di Nemi
description: A Roman Villa on the shores of Lago di Nemi.

name: Villas at Castelporziano
description: Since antiquity, the 6,000-ha estate at Castelporziano has been an elite seaside compound. Where a modern pleasure villa for the Italian president now sits are remains of Roman villas on the Laurentine shore; these remains have been the object of continued study since 1983.

name: Unnamed Roman villa
description: A villa rustica located near the coast, southwest of Ardea.

name: Unnamed Roman villa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D3 no. 57 (villa near Norba)

name: Roman villa near Setia
description: The platform, including cryptoporticus, of a republican period villa originally dating to the first century B.C. with evidence for second century A.D. renovations.

name: Acqua Claudia
description: A Roman villa at Anguillara Sabazia that may have been built in order to take advantage of mineral springs. The villa dates to the Late Republican period but has only been partially excavated. A large hemicycle with ambulatio is extant; this feature may have been coordinated with the villa's garden.

name: Astura
description: Astura was once a coastal island of Latium and is now a small peninsula at the southeastern edge of the Bay of Antium.

name: Villa Maritima di Capo di Sorrento
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 F4 Capo di Sorrento

name: Damecuta
description: An Imperial villa excavated by A. Maiuri in the 1930s, Damecuta was connected to the Iulio-Claudian emperors. The construction of later Medieval and Bourbon fortifications on the site significantly damaged the ancient remains. The Medieval Damecuta tower stands atop part of the Roman villa.

name: Grottacce
description: At Grottacce are the remains of a Roman seaside villa or villa maritima.

name: Lago di Paola
description: A villa of the emperor Domitian located in the area of Circeii.

name: Matrice
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 G2 Matrice

name: Minori
description: Minori is the site of a major villa located on the Amalfi coast, some 11 km WSW of Salerno. Destroyed by a possible landslide, the villa was in use from the Iulio-Claudian period through to the Flavian period.

name: Mura di S. Stefano
description: A second century A.D. Roman villa built atop an earlier villa rustica.

name: Neratii
description: This site of an imperial villa, dating to the first and second centuries, covers an area of ca. 15,000 sq. m. It is located in the southern portion of the modern civic territory of San Giuliano del Sannio in the Província de Campobasso, Italy.

name: Palazzo a Mare
description: The site of one of the twelve villas of Tiberius, and perhaps the largest, on the island of Capreae.

name: Roman villa at Positano
description: An opulent Roman villa maritima destroyed by the A.D. 79 eruption of Vesuvius. The remains of an opulent and well-decorated Roman villa have been known at Positano since at least 1758 when ruins were discovered during work on the bell tower of the church of S. Maria Assunta. Karl Weber conducted excavations there in the same year. Modern work, begun in 1999, has stabilized and consolidated the site.

name: Punta Eolo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 D4 Punta Eolo

name: S. Giacomo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 G2 S. Giacomo

name: S. Rocco
description: The San Rocco villa was built during the second quarter of the first century B.C. It was initially a simple villa rustica and it was later enlarged substantially.

name: Sava di Baronissi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 G4 Sava di Baronissi

name: Scauri
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E3 Scauri

name: Torre S. Limato
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E3 Torre S. Limato

name: Treppete
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E3 Treppete

name: Villa Euchelia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E2 Villa Euchelia

name: Villa Iovis
description: A 7,000 m² palatial villa built atop the island of Capreae by the Roman emperor Tiberius.

name: Villa Tiberii
description: A coastal villa at Sperlonga associated with the Roman emperor Tiberius.

name: Agnuli
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 D1 Agnuli

name: Atella
description: The site of a Roman villa in the locality of Magnone (Potenza) in South Italy. The archaeological site was first identified in 1979. Limited excavations identified a storage complex housing dolia. The presence of African Red Slip pottery suggests a date in the fourth century.

name: Avicenna
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 C1 Avicenna

name: Ciccotti, S. Gilio
description: A first century BC Roman villa in Basilicata.

name: Gaudiano
description: Remains of a large villa that was in use from the Augustan period to the seventh century A.D.

name: Lago del Lupo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 E4 Lago del Lupo

name: Moltone, S. Pietro
description: San Pietro di Tolve Roman villa.

name: Pattano
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B4 Pattano

name: Pietrastretta
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 C3 Pietrastretta

name: Posta Crusta
description: The 'villa rustica' at Posta Crusta.

name: Saltus Carminianensis
description: The Saltus Carminianensis was a large imperial property of northern Apulia mentioned in the Notitia Dignitatum Occidentis. Its precise location and borders are not known today.

name: Villa di Monte delle Colonnacce
description: A Roman villa located near Castel di Guido.

name: Villa of the Papyri
description: A suburban villa located northwest of Herculaneum that was originally owned by Lucius Calpurnius Piso Caesoninus.

name: Villa of the Mysteries
description: A Roman villa located northwest of Pompeii.

name: Dios Hieron?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F2 Dios Hieron?

name: Villa Romana di Casalotti
description: A villa site that includes a bathing complex, dating from the second to fourth centuries CE.

name: Villa di Faonte a Vigne Nuove
description: The so-called the Villa of Faonte was a Roman villa in Rome located between Via delle Vigne Nuove and Via Passo del Turchino. There are remains of a cistern (approx. 29.5 by 14 meters) now set in a garden area.

name: Colle Lecinone
description: Remains of a villa built on several terraces.

name: Bollendorf
description: Roman villa rustica in Bollendorf

name: Longuich
description: Roman villa urbana in Longuich

name: Villa Romana di S. Andrea
description: The partially preserved structural remains of an Imperial villa dating to the third and fourth centuries.

name: Casa Bellezza
description: The so-called "Casa Bellezza" is a Roman domus located on the flank of the Aventine Hill in Rome. Its remains are found at Largo Arrigo VII, no. 2-4.

name: Casa della statuetta indiana
description: The so-called "Casa della statuetta indiana" (I.8.5) lies on the Via dell'Abbondanza. Excavated between 1930 and 1935, it was named for the fine ivory statuette (thought by some to depict Lakshmi) found inside. The house has a slightly irregular floor plan with some rooms added to the structure from adjacent properties.

name: House of Augustus
description: The House of Augustus on the Palatine Hill in Rome, ca. 36 BCE.

name: Saint-Pierre de Vence
description: A Gallo-Roman villa.

name: Vivios
description: Remains of a Roman villa.

name: Espelt Roman villa
description: A Roman villa that was inhabited from the late first century AD until the fifth century AD.

name: Villa del Pastore
description: A large villa at Stabiae covering more than 17,000 square meters.

name: Villa del fondo Ippolito Zurlo
description: A Roman villa located in the contrada Giuliana at Boscoreale on a side street of via Grotta II. The site was excavated by Vincenzo De Prisco in 1897 and subsequently reinterred.

name: Villa of Asellius
description: A Roman villa excavated at Boscoreale in 1903 and 1904 under the supervision of Vincenzo De Prisco and located on the property of one Giuseppe De Martino. Wall paintings and artifacts were recovered from the site, and the villa was subsequently reinterred. Records as to its precise location are less than optimal.

name: Mülheim-Kärlich
description: A Roman villa dating to the second and third centuries A.D.

name: Remstecken
description: Site of a Roman villa rustica, dating from the first to third centuries AD.

name: Haselburg
description: A second-century AD Roman villa estate.

name: Villa delle Colonne
description: A late Republican and early Imperial villa in the ager Cosanus noted for its turreted enclosure wall.

name: Roman Villa La Grottaccia
description: The remains of a Roman villa (first to fourth centuries CE) at Ladispoli. The surviving cryptoporticus gives the site its name.

name: Villa Marittima della Tagliata
description: A maritime villa of the first century A.D. adjacent to the port of Cosa.

name: La Pièce du Fient
description: An elite Gallo-Roman villa with evidence for elaborate gardens dating from the first century CE, now located near the modern French town of Richebourg (Ile-de-France).

name: Rutland Roman Villa
description: A Roman villa, identified and initially excavated since 2018, located in the modern district of Rutland (England, United Kingdom), east of Foster's Bridge and southwest of Ketton.

name: Clelia Roman Villa
description: A Late Roman villa and necropolis located at modern Imola in Italy.

name: House of the Gilded Cupids
description: A Roman domus that once belonged to Cn. Poppaeus Habitus (VI 16,7).

name: Villa Arcinelli
description: The remains of a Roman platform villa, the first phase of which belongs to the first century BCE.

name: Jonvelle
description: The ruins of a Gallo-Roman villa with polychrome mosaic pavement.

name: House of the Red Walls
description: A domus excavated in 1832 and 1882. It is noted for its late-stage wall decoations that may call upon public monuments for inspiration.

name: House of Marcus Lucretius
description: The House of Marcus Lucretius was excavated in 1846 and 1851.

name: Casa di Properzio
description: The so-called "Casa di Properzio" at Assisi is a Roman domus located near the southern curtain of the ancient city walls. The decorations date the house to the first century B.C.

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 G2 Tixover

name: Tockington
description: Site of a Roman villa excavated in 1887-8. The site was an enclosed courtyard, with a main house, hypocaust, and baths, as well as an aisled farmhouse.

name: Totternhoe Roman Villa
description: This Roman courtyard villa that yielded ceramics dating to the 4th century CE was located on what is now Church Farm in Totternhoe (Central Bedfordshire, England). The survey and excavations were published in the 1950s; the site was backfilled after excavation and the villa's remains are no longer visible.

name: Walesby
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 G1 Walesby

name: Walton-on-the-Hill
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 G3 Walton-on-the-Hill

name: Wanstead
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 H3 Wanstead

name: Wellow
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 E3 Wellow

name: Wendens Ambo
description: Remains of a first through fourth-century corridor villa were excavated at Wendens Ambo in 1853 and 1971-3. The villa had a detached bathhouse and other out buildings.

name: West Blatchington
description: An aisled farmhouse dating to the third century and measuring ca. 35 x 15 m.

name: West Dean
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 F3 West Dean

name: West Langton
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 G2 West Langton

name: West Marden
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 G4 West Marden

name: West Meon
description: A small Romano-British courtyard villa was excavated at Lippen Wood, West Meon, by A. Moray-Williams in 1906. The villa had 16 rooms, three of which had mosaic floors.

name: White Staunton
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 D4 White Staunton

name: Whittington
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 F3 Whittington

name: Whittlebury
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 G2 Whittlebury

name: Whitton
description: Whitton was the site of a pre-Roman fortified farmstead that gave way to a humble Roman farm. Abandoned ca. A.D. 350.

name: Castle Hill, Whitton
description: A large villa complex located at Castle Hill, Whitton. This site is considered to be one of the most important villa complexes known in Suffolk. Excavations were conducted in 1929-32, 1946-50 and 1988-9.

name: Wick
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 E3 Wick

name: Wingham
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 I3 Wingham

name: Winterton
description: Winterton is the site of a Roman villa and field system. The ancient site, now invisible, is located just to the north of Scunthorpe, Lincolnshire U.K.

name: Withington
description: The Roman-British corridor villa at Withington was discovered in 1811. The site is located to the south of Withington village along with another Romano-British building/villa complex.

name: Woodchester
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 E3 Woodchester

name: Woolstone
description: Excavations of this villa site revealed mosaics, the remains of baths, and a corridor centered around a courtyard.

name: Worplesdon
description: Worplesdon is the site of a Roman corridor-style villa, probably dating from the third to fourth century CE.

name: Yarchester
description: A corridor villa, occupied by A.D. 200, that was part of a complex of buildings arranged around a courtyard.

name: Yeovil
description: In 1925, Alderman Mitchelmore excavated a Roman courtyard villa at Yeovil. This site is located to the west of Seaton Road, Westland, Yeovil. Excavations continued in 1927/8 under Ralegh Radford. On September 15, 1916, a coin hoard containing more than 4,000 fourth-century CE coins was found on the site. The villa was a large building (200 by 170 feet) with a paved courtyard.

name: Casa del Fabbro
description: Casa del Fabbro or House of the Cabinet Maker is a domus located in Pompeii's Regio I. It derives its modern appellation from an extensive set of carpentry tools found in situ during excavation. The site was excavated in 1914, 1927, and 1933.

name: House of the Ocean Gods
description: Large house in Saint-Romain-En-Gaul, France, probably belonging to a wealthy merchant, built around 50 CE and abandoned in the middle of the 3rd century CE.

name: Villa of Masseria Ciccotti
description: A Roman villa along the via Herculea near Oppido Lucano.

name: Domus della Fortuna Annonaria
description: The so-called "Domus della Fortuna Annonaria" (V,II,8) is an ancient peristyle house with a nymphaeum located in Ostia. Its initial construction likely dates to the reign of Antoninus Pius.

name: Prato La Corte
description: A villa rustica was excavated near Licenza in 1913, the exact position of which is no longer known. This site included a possible granary room, as well as a torcular.

name: Turkdean
description: Site of a major Roman villa complex in Gloucestershire, active from late second century CE until the end of the fourth century.

name: Villa Cardillio
description: Remains of a Roman villa located in the modern Torres Novas Municipality, Santarém District, Portugal.

name: Rio Maior
description: A Roman villa in Rio Maior, Santarém, Portugal.

name: East Creech villa
description: A Roman villa with mosaic floors and columns was discovered between East Creech and Cotness in 1869.

name: Collet Roman Villa
description: A Roman villa has been identified in the modern neighborhood of El Collet, which is located in the Catalonian municipality of Calonge i Sant Antoni.

name: Pagus Augustus Felix Suburbanus
description: The ancient Pagus Augustus Felix Suburbanus corresponds to the territory of today's city of Boscoreale and Boscotrecase. Originating around 80 B.C. with the name of Pagus Felix Suburbanus, it included many farms.

name: House of Plotilla
description: The House of Plotilla, also known as Casa di Apollo e Coronide, was excavated in 1815 and again in 1839. It lies in Regio VIII of Pompeii.

name: Domus Lucina
description: The Domus Lucina was located on the Via Lata, which is the site where a certain widowed matrona named Lucina harbored Marcellus I and then established a church in his name.

name: Marigny à Fleurville Gallo-Roman Villa
description: Remains of a Gallo-Roman Villa at modern Château de Marigny at Fleurville, France.

name: Villa Romana di Punta San Limato
description: The ruins of a Roman villa located in the modern Italian locality of Punta San Limato, province of Caserta.

name: House of the Cryptoporticus at Vulci
description: A well-appointed Roman domus with a large, well-preserved cryptoporticus. The earliest phase belongs to the late second and early first century B.C. with subsequent modifications during the early Imperial period.

name: Maison des Nones de Mars
description: A large, centrally located Roman townhouse in Augustoritum (modern Limoges), built in the first century CE and decorated with elaborate wall paintings.

name: Unnamed villa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 E4 unnamed villa (in N Cos)

name: Domus and Mitreo delle Pareti Dipinte
description: The complex known as "Domus and Mitreo delle Pareti Dipinte" began its life in the later second century BCE and was remodeled during the time of Augustus. A mithraeum is built in the northwest part of the building in the later second century CE.

name: Villa di via della Magliana
description: The remains of a Roman villa on land that is now mostly consumed by a golf course connected to a hotel resort.

name: La Befa, Carcerelle
description: The Roman villa rustica of La Befa is positioned at the point where the Torrente di Crevoli joins the river Ombrone. Excavation of the site identified a northern sector and a southern sector, both with multiple architectural phases, likely beginning the later first century CE.

name: So-called House of the Doric Peristyle
description: A large private house in Cyrene.

name: Domus Clementis
description: The remains of a private domus that date to the second and third centuries CE and include a Mithraeum. This complex eventually became incorporated into the fourth-century basilica on the site of the later basilica of San Clemente al Laterano.

name: Little Oakley Roman villa
description: The site of a Roman villa that was partially excavated in 1939 and then again from 1951 to 1978. The site was occupied from ca. A.D. 60 until the fourth century.

name: Rabaçal Roman villa
description: A fourth-century AD Roman villa located in Rabaçal, Portugal, near the site of Conimbriga.

name: Dar Buk-Ammarah
description: A coastal villa known especially for its polychrome gladiatorial mosaics was excavated at Dar Buk-Ammarah, located on the coast some 4.5 km to the north of Zliten, Libya.

name: Cancello e Arnone
description: The ruins of a Roman villa of the ager Falernus were discovered at Cancello e Arnone during work on a green energy facility.

name: House of the cithara player
description: The so-called "House of the cithara player" (I,4,5 and 25) opens both on to the Via Stabiana and the Via dell'Abbondanza. One of the largest aristocratic homes in Pompeii, it was formed from two adjoining properties.

name: Domus of Titus Macer
description: The House of Titus Macer at Aquileia dates to the middle of the first century CE when at least two pre-exisiting domus were merged to form a single domus covering more than 1,500 square meters.

name: Cuarto Roble
description: A fortified Roman villa within the territory of Norba Caesarina. Re-occupation occurred during the Islamic period in Spain.

name: Villa romana di via Carciano
description: A Roman villa dating to the second century A.D.

name: Villa di Formello
description: A Roman villa dating ca. 50 B.C. located near Monte Gennaro.

name: Probatas
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 G4 Probatas

name: Soleta
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 H4 Soleta

name: House of the Sundial at Baelo
description: House at Baelo Claudia located across the colonnaded street from the House of the West.

name: Villa Augustea
description: The so-called “Villa of Augustus” at Somma Vesuviana was first discovered in the late nineteenth century. The villa has been shown to have survived the 79 CE eruption of Mt. Vesuvius but was eventually destroyed by a volcanic event in 472.

name: Byzantine house
description: A Byzantine house located at Scylletium/Scolacium.

name: Paturro Roman villa
description: A luxury terraced villa in Spain's Murcia region that was first built in the Republican period and then abandoned by the third century A.D.

name: Nossa Senhora da Tourega
description: An imperial villa located in the territory of Ebora.

name: Priddy
description: A Roman building located approximately 270 meters north and east of Priddy Church. The site, excavated in 1964, has been interpreted as being part of a small villa site.

name: Roman villa
description: Remains of an early imperial period Roman villa, the so-called "Domus della Madonna delle Grazie".

name: Pleshey
description: A possible Roman courtyard villa has been identified at Pleshey, based on the discovery of mosaic pavements, burials, and coin finds, most of which were recorded by ploughing in the 18th century.

name: Cignana
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 D4 Cignana

name: Cozzo Cicirello
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 E4 Cozzo Cicirello

name: Cusumano
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 C3 Cusumano

name: Durrueli di Realmonte
description: A Roman villa rustica dating to the first century A.D. located in the Punta Grande beach, in the territory of Realmonte, province of Agrigento, Sicily.

name: Gerace Roman Villa
description: A fourth century A.D. Roman villa with mosaic pavements. The villa may have extended to some 10,000 square meters in area.

name: Roman Villa at Għajn Tuffieħa, Malta
description: Ruins at this ancient site include a Roman-era bath complex and extensive mosaics. Its ancient name is unknown.

name: Grancifone
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 D4 Grancifone

name: Golata Niva
description: An archaeological site near the village of Sinemorets. The site of a Thracian aristocratic woman's home, dated to the third to second centuries BCE.

name: House of Mikion and Menon
description: A house located southwest of the Agora in Athens, which was built ca. 475-450 BC and demolished ca. 275 BC. It was used by marble workers, including the sculptors Mikion and Menon. The courtyard contained two cisterns, known as the Demeter Cistern and Menon's Cistern.

name: Rudston Roman Villa
description: a fourth-century Romano-British villa west of the modern village of Rudston

name: Saint-Nizier-sous-Charlieu
description: The site of a Gallo-Roman villa known since 1884.

name: Villa Rustica, Loerrach-Brombach
description: Ruins of a villa rustica, located south of modern Brombach in Baden-Württemberg.

name: Domus della Soglia nilotica
description: A domus at Privernum dating to the early Imperial period and noted for its mosaic pavements.

name: Thurnham Roman villa
description: A second century Roman villa located near Thurnham, Kent, United Kingdom

name: Manor House Roman villa
description: The site of a Roman corridor villa with a separate bathhouse. Three phases dating from the late first century AD into the fourth century. It possibly forms part of a complex with the villa uncovered ca. 250 meters to the south and east.

name: Valdonega Roman villa
description: The Roman villa of Valdonega near Verona dates to the first century A.D.

name: House of Aulus Umbricius Scaurus
description: The House of Aulus Umbricius Scaurus (Regio VII, Insula 16, entrances 12-15) is considered to be the residence and trading center of the gens Umbricia, a Pompeian family involved in producing and selling garum. The house itself is described as a "Samnite-type" house and in the first century B.C. it was most likely owned by the Samnite military commander Maras Spurnius who resisted the invasion of Sulla.

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 E4 El Castillet

name: Via Gabina villas
description: Two suburban villa sites and a Late Antique granary were excavated at a site along the Via Gabina 14 km east of central Rome. Walter Widrig and Philip Oliver-Smith directed the excavations.

name: Colombarone
description: An archaeological site that preserves remains of a Late Roman settlement along the Via Flaminia.

name: Listercombe Bottom
description: The site of a Roman villa discovered ca. 1760 and excavated in 1930. It lies ca. 2.5 km SE of Chedworth Roman villa.

name: Romano-British villa 250m south of Park Farm
description: A Romano-British villa and its associated features are located to the north-east of the River Ingol and along the ancient route known as the Icknield Way.

name: Domus Romane di Palazzo Valentini
description: The remains of a Roman domus and a bath complex adjacent to the area of the Forum of Trajan.

name: Roman villa at Mill End
description: The remains of a Romano-British villa on the Buckinghamshire bank of the River Thames just south of the village of Mill End. The remains were first observed via parchmarks in 1921.

name: Villa in via Enzo Tortora
description: A villa site that was active from the middle Republican period to the early Middle Ages was investigated in 2009 and 2010 at via Enzo Tortora, no. 79. The site is located about 500 meters east of the line of the ancient Via Flaminia.

name: House of Argus
description: The House of Argus at Herculaneum is located on the west side of Cardo III adjacent to the House of Artistides.

name: Unnamed place
description: An unnamed monument, settlement, or villa of Syrtica

name: Voconius Pollio villa
description: A Roman villa commonly associated with Q. Voconius Pollio that was excavated in the nineteenth century.

name: House of Julius Polybius
description: The House of Julius Polybius (IX 13,1.3) sits on the north side of the Via dell'Abbondanza and dates from the second century BC.

name: House of Venus in the shell
description: Located on the Via dell'Abbondanza, the so-called "House of Venus in the shell" is also known as the House of D. Lucretius Satrius Valens.

name: House of the geometric mosaics
description: The House of the Geometric Mosaics (VIII,2,14-16) is a large domus of more than 60 rooms that was achieved by combining two third to second century BC houses.

name: Villa del Cimitero Flaminio
description: A Roman villa located at the 1.6 km post of the via Tiberina, positioned on a tuff bank, today inside the Prima Porta cemetery.

name: Massaciuccoli Romana - Padiglione "Guglielmo Lera"
description: The site of a Roman imperial-period villa in the modern Italian comune of Massaciuccoli Romana.

name: Harpole Roman villa
description: A Roman villa north of Panattoni Park and adjacent to Junction 16 of the M1 motorway, excavated in the 1840s and in 1966.

name: Ioukoundianai/Sekoundianai
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 53 A3 Ioukoundianai/Sekoundianai

name: Sophianai
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 53 B2 Sophianai

name: Nea Skala
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 C5 Nea Skala

name: Vatsa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 B5 Vatsa

name: Villa of Lucius Nonius Calpurnius Torquatus Asprenas
description: The remains of a Roman villa on the Via Ostiense have been linked to Lucius Nonius Calpurnius Torquatus Asprenas.

name: House of Paquius Proculus
description: The House of Paquius Proculus at Pompeii, also known as the House of C. Cuspius Pansa, is a domus which was excavated in 1911 and then from 1923 to 1926.

name: Unnamed villa
description: An unnamed villa of Cyrenaica

name: Banjole Roman villa
description: A Roman maritime villa located at the southwest corner of the Istrian peninsula, about six kilometers south of Pola. According to Matijašić 1982, the remains of the villa are visible beneath the waters of Paltana Bay.

name: Concarneau
description: Site of a Gallo-Roman villa discovered in 1964 in the village of Le Questel near Concarneau. The villa dates from the middle of the first century BCE to the fourth century CE.

name: Colleville
description: An unusual Gallo-Roman dwelling was excavated during the 1960s. Its main periods of occupation corresponded to the reign of Augustus and then the second century A.D.

name: House of Dionysos at Paphos
description: Roman villa at Nea Paphos on Cyprus containing extensive mosaic floors.

name: Almoinhas Roman Villa
description: A Roman villa located near Loures, dating to the first through fifth centuries A.D.

name: So-called House of Hesychios
description: A Roman peristyle house located in Cyrene.

props.ancientName: House of Hesychios, House of Hesychius

name: House of Aufidia Cornelia Valentinilla
description: Archaeological excavations in the area of the Aqua Marcia and the Basilica of the Holy Cross in Jerusalem (Rome) in 1887 and 1888 uncovered the remains of lead water pipes identifying this domus as that of one Aufidia Cornelia Valentinilla.

name: Domus dei Ritratti
description: The so-called "Domus dei Ritratti" is located in the archaeological area surrounding Santa Croce in Gerusalemme in Rome. The domus dates to the later Roman Empire.

name: villa del Cavalcavia di Salone
description: The so-called "Villa of the Jurist" was discovered in 2013 during salvage excavations connected with the expansion of Italy's A24 motorway. A significant villa of the Late Republican period was documented and attributed to the jurist Quintus Mucius Scaevola.

name: Monte Gentili
description: A villa associated with Aulus Vitellius Germanicus.

name: Unnamed Roman villa
description: The cryptoporticus of an Augustan-period villa that later came to be incorporated in the substructure of the abbey of Santa Maria Maddalena in Armillis.

name: House of Anicetus
description: The House of Actius Anicetus (I.3.23) at Pompeii is best known as the site where the fresco painting depicting the battle between the Nucerians and the Pompeians in the amphitheater of Pompeii was discovered.

name: Mettet
description: A Gallo-Roman villa located in the vicinity of Bauselenne.

name: House of Romulus and Remus
description: The so-called House of Romulus and Remus in Pompeii is a domus located at VII.7.10. The site was originally linked to VII.7.13 and was excavated in 1859 and again in 1864. During World War II, the site was bombed and significantly damaged on August 24, 1943, and September 13, 1943. Many of the extant wall paintings in the house were destroyed.

name: Dryhill Roman villa
description: Romano-British villa situated in present-day Gloucestershire

name: Franks Roman villa
description: The site of a Roman villa with two parallel ranges of rooms dating from the second half of the first century to the fifth century.

name: Lillyhorn Roman villa
description: The site of a 29-room Romano-British villa located in present-day Gloucestershire.

name: So-called House of Jason Magnus
description: A very large Roman house on the acropolis ridge of Cyrene.

name: House of the Mosaic Atrium
description: A domus in Herculaneum. The mosaic "chessboard" decoration of its atrium distinguishes it from neighboring domus.

name: Villa Poppaea
description: A seaside villa located in Oplontis, possibly owned by Emperor Nero, based on decorations, date of construction, and pottery fragments inscribed with the name of a freedman of Poppaea Sabina.

name: Borg Roman villa
description: A large Roman courtyard villa covering ca. 7.5 ha.

name: House L7-H
description: Private house later adapted for use as synagogue phase 1.

name: House M8-A
description: Private house preceding the Christian church.

name: House J1-A
description: Remains of a large house with peristyle located within the intramuros military sector of Dura-Europos.

name: House K5-A
description: Private house converted later into the barracks of K5.

name: House X7-A
description: Private house later converted for military use as X7 Barracks.

name: House X7-B
description: Private house later converted for military use as X7 Barracks.

name: House H2-D
description: Private house located in Block H2 of Dura-Europos.

name: Roman villa on viale Alfredo Ottaviani
description: The ruins of a Roman villa were discovered along viale Alfredo Ottaviani at Acilia Nord in 2011.

name: Posta Vecchia
description: Remains of a Roman imperial villa incorporated into a post office that later became a hotel.

name: House of the Gorgons
description: The so-called House of the Gorgons at Ostia originally dates to the late republican period. It has an unusual trapezoidal plan and is divided into 17 rooms.

name: San Potito Sannitico
description: A Roman villa in the locality of Torelle at San Potito Sannitico.

name: Masseria De Carolis
description: A Roman villa with an associated bath complex destroyed by the AD 79 eruption of Vesuvius, the Masseria De Carolis site is located in the comune of Pollena Trocchia near Naples, Italy.

name: Whitton Lodge Roman Villa
description: Whitton Lodge is the site of a later Prehistoric settlement enclosure that developed into a Roman villa. The site was identified in the twentieth century by means of cropmarks. Nothing is visible at the surface today.

name: Dolia Roman Villa
description: A Roman villa site now inundated by the coastal waters of the Saronic Gulf near Palaia Epidavros, Argolis.

name: Grotte di Catullo
description: Located on the Sirmione peninsula on the southern shore of Lake Garda, "Grotte di Catullo" is the name given to the ruins of a Roman villa dating to the late first century B.C. and the beginning of the first century A.D.

name: Ramla bay villa
description: A villa maritima discovered in 1910-11.

name: House of Many Colors
description: A large and well-appointed house located in the so-called "Villa Section" of ancient Olynthus, proposed as an Olynthian type-house.

name: Tserli
description: The foundations of a Hellenistic farm located near the shores of Lake Boibe in Thessaly.

name: House of Marcus Lucretius Fronto
description: The House of M. Lucretius Fronto (V, 4, a, 11), situated off the north side of the Via di Nola, is an example of a refined domus that is small in size but with opulent Third and Fourth style wall paintings.

name: The House of the Masks
description: The House of the Masks (also known as the House of the Comedians), built around 150-100 BC, was a luxurious residential complex that may have housed traveling troops of actors performing at the theater in Delos.

name: House of Livia
description: The so-called "House of Livia" on the Palatine Hill in Rome is a multi-phase domus with both Republican and Imperial phases.

name: Unnamed domus in Via Graziosa
description: A domus excavated in Via Graziosa in Rome, Italy (now the Via Cavour) on 7 April 1848 produced a sequence of wall paintings depicting scenes from Homer's Odyssey.

name: Casale dell’Infernaccio
description: A Roman villa located at Magliana Vecchia that dates to the second century A.D.

name: Domus of Publius Confuleius Sabbio
description: The domus of the freedman P. Confuleius Sabbio at Capua.

name: Villa urbana
description: A Roman villa that has now been reconstructed.

name: House of the Hotel
description: The so-called House of the Hotel (Casa dell'Albergo) is an opulent Augustan-period domus that occupies the bulk of the southern end of Insula III at Herculaneum.

name: House of Aristides
description: A domus at Herculaneum that earned its modern sobriquet thanks to the misidentification of a statue found in its ruins. The statue in question was thought to be Aristides, one of the ten Attic orators, when, in fact, it is a statue of Aeschines.

name: Roman villa at West Mersea
description: Remains of a tessellated floor pavement likely indicate the presence of a villa.

name: Roman villa at Maasbracht
description: A Roman villa near the modern village of Maasbracht, Limburg, Netherlands, famous for its murals. The earliest structures on the site are dated to the second century CE, and the villa was abandoned by the middle of the third century.

name: Casa dell'Impluvium at Rusellae
description: The so-called "Casa dell'Impluvium" at Rusellae was excavated in the 1980s. It may present an important, early example of an Italic courtyard house. It dates to the sixth century B.C.

name: Roman Town House, Dorchester
description: A Romano-British house located within Colliton Park, Dorchester, Dorset.

name: Costa Murata archaeological area
description: A site with votive activity from the Archaic period and a later Roman house in addition to the basalt-paved Roman road.

name: Casa del pomarius Felix
description: This house (I.8.2) opens onto the Via dell'Abbondanza and is connected to the taberna at I.8.1 and the shop at I.8.3. It was named the "Casa del pomarius Felix" from electoral programmata painted on its facade. It has a typical atrium-peristyle plan.

name: Maison romaine du Parking Pasteur, Aix-en-Provence
description: A well-appointed Roman domus was discovered at the site of what is now a parking structure in Aix-en-Provence.

name: Villa Romana Baláca
description: The site of a Roman villa that was discovered in 1904 in the Balácapuszta field of Vámos village in western Hungary.

name: House of the Surgeon (Rimini)
description: The so-called House of the Surgeon in Rimini may have been the home of a Roman doctor by the name of Eutyches.

name: Villa di Podere Anna
description: The ruins of a Roman villa rustica were discovered in the 1990s along via Diego Fabbri. The site dates to the first century B.C. and remained in use at least until the fifth century A.D. The remains of a bath complex are substantial.

name: Saint-Saturnin-du-Bois Gallo-Roman villa
description: Remains of an extensive Gallo-Roman villa located in the modern French commune of Saint-Saturnin-du-Bois (Charente-Maritime department).

name: Casa dell’ Amore punito
description: A domus excavated in 1844 and 1867.

name: Untitled
description: Unlabeled feature captured from the Barrington Atlas

name: Untitled
description: Unlabeled feature captured from the Barrington Atlas

name: Capo di Bove
description: An archaeological site located at the fourth milestone of the Via Appia Antica.

name: Valbandon Roman Villa(s)
description: Architectural remains consistent with a Roman-era villa maritima have been identified on both the north and south sides of a small bay at modern Valbandon in Croatia. Originally thought to represent a single establishment that wrapped the entire bay, more recent interpretations would see two separate architectural complexes.

description: The remains of a Roman villa dating to the first century CE were discovered in the Botzdorf district of the city of Bornheim in the Rhein-Sieg district of Germany in 2002. It is also referred to as the "Villa Fortuna".

name: Chesterton villa
description: A Roman villa located north of Ewefields Farm was partially excavated in the 1990s at Chesterton, United Kingdom.

name: House of the Golden Bracelet
description: The so-called "House of the Golden Bracelet" is a domus at Pompeii located on the Vicolo del Farmacista. It takes its name from the discovery of the remains of a woman wearing a thick gold bracelet.

name: Miersch
description: A Roman villa rustica discovered at Miersch in the early twentieth century. It was abandoned ca. 400 CE.

name: House of Leda and the Swan

props.ancientName: House of Leda and the Swan

name: Vacone Roman villa
description: A Roman villa site near Rieti known initially from several cryptoporticus structures. More recent excavations have revealed a series of rooms, including some with mosaic pavement.

name: Archaeological area of Villa di Campetti
description: The site of a Roman villa complex covering ca. 10,000 square meters. The villa was built in the late republican period (second-first centuries B.C.) on the hillside adjacent to the Archaic Portonaccio sanctuary.

name: Crofton Roman villa
description: A villa occupied between ca. AD 140 and 400 now located in Orpington, in the London Borough of Bromley.

name: Frielas
description: A Roman villa.

name: Quinta da Bolacha
description: A Roman villa located near several productive sites.

name: Villa dos Casais Velhos
description: The remains of a fortified Roman villa.

name: Altheim-Weirading villa rustica
description: A Roman "villa rustica" occupied from ca. 80 - 240 CE, located in the Innviertel region, northeast of the Austrian town of Altheim, village of Weirading (Kiriberg district). This is one of three such sites in the general vicinity of Altheim.

name: Malvaccaro Roman villa
description: An imperial Roman villa located near Potenza.

name: Domus dei Coiedii
description: The so-called Domus dei Coiedii is a domus located at Suasa. It dates to the first century B.C.

name: House of the Vettii
description: The so-called House of the Vettii (VI, 15, 1) is a luxurious townhouse (domus) located in Pompeii. The house's owners in its final phase were the freedmen brothers Aulus Vettius Conviva, an Augustalis, and Aulus Vettius Restitutus. Among other features. the house is important for its Fourth Style wall paintings.

name: Villa del Vergigno, Montelupo Fiorentino
description: A large villa rustica inhabited from ca. 100 B.C. to A.D. 500, located near Montelupo Fiorentino in the mid-Arno Valley of northern Tuscany, approximately twenty km west of Florence.

name: Marina di Palo Roman villa
description: An Imperial maritime villa located in Ladispoli, Italy.

name: Roman villa at Santa Lucia
description: A villa constructed in the 4th century CE.

name: Domus of Salvius
description: A well-appointed domus of the first century CE located on the Molinete hill in Cartagena, Spain.

name: Boxted Roman villa
description: A Roman villa excavated in the late 1880s and dating from the late first century CE to the late second century at Boxted, Upchurch, United Kingdom

name: Northfleet Villa
description: A Roman villa complex near Dartford, dating from the first to fourth centuries CE. The archaeological remains of the villa, as well as other finds dating from the paleolithic to modern times, were found in the area now occupied by and surrounding the Ebbsfleet International railway station.

name: So-called Area H in Berenike
description: The ruins of a house of the early Roman period at the archaeological site of Sidi Khiribech, in Berenike. The house had a courtyard with a peristyle. Some of the internal walls were decorated with frescoes representing the Muses.

name: Vižula Maritime Villa
description: An extensive maritime villa of the Roman period occupied the western and southern coasts of the Vižula peninsula, located near modern Medulin in Croatia.

name: House of the Painters at Work
description: The House of the Painters at Work or Casa dei pittori al lavoro is a ruined domus at Pompeii that was undergoing renovations and repairs at the time of the city's final destruction.

props.ancientName: House of the Painters at Work

name: Noheda Roman villa

name: Villa Romana de los Villaricos
description: The site of Villaricos lies 5 km east of Mula (Murcia), Spain. The Roman villa at the site was large and well-appointed, including rich mosaics and a bathing complex. There is also an industrial area that includes a torcularium (facility for pressing of olives or grapes). The site's use ranges from the first to the fifth century CE.
