name: House of the Skeleton
description: The so-called "The House of the Skeleton" at Herculaneum is a Roman domus that owes its name to the discovery of a human skeleton during excavations. Carlo Bonucci initially explored it between 1830 and 1831. Amedeo Maiuri completed the excavation in 1927.

name: House of Butehamon
description: House of the scribe Butehamon in the enclosure of the funerary temple of Ramses III at Medinet Habu, near the western fortified gate.

name: House of Pansa
description: The House of Pansa (VI,6,1) is an atrium house in Pompeii that occupies an entire insula. Architectural elements date the structure ca. 140-120 BC in its original phase. The merchant and duumvir Cn. Alleius Nigidius Maius owned the property.

name: Villa near Wadi Ghirza
description: An ancient villa near the Wadi Ghirza

name: Poggio la Guardia
description: The site of a Roman villa.

name: L'Alcora
description: A Roman villa.

name: Montione
description: A large villa platform, perhaps measuring 150 meters square, located near Grottaferrata, southeast of Rome. The earliest phase belonged to the first century B.C.

name: House of the Deep Trench at Italica
description: House inside the city wall, located on a street that leads directly onto the east end of the Traianeum, the great temple erected in honor of the emperor Trajan.

name: Casa di Bacco
description: The House of Bacchus at Pompeii takes its name from a wall painting featuring the god Bacchus. Excavations were carried out in 1824 and 1826. The site was bombed during World War II in 1943.

name: Domus of A. Octavius Primus
description: The so-called House of A. Octavius Primus was excavated in 1859 and again in 1872.

name: House of the Silver Wedding
description: The so-called House of the Silver Wedding was built in the second century BCE and remodeled in the first century CE. It is noted for its particularly elegant tetrastyle atrium executed in the Corinthian order. Excavated between 1891 and 1893, it earned its modern moniker due to the celebration of the 'silver wedding' of Umberto and Margherita of Savoy in 1893. The domus was extensively reconstructed in the late nineteenth century using then-current methods. These reconstructions weathered poorly, and a modern restoration project to stabilize and conserve the domus, in effect removing the harmful earlier restorations, was completed in 2023.

name: Fingrinhoe Roman Site
description: A multi-acre site, now located within the bounds of the Fingringhoe Wick Nature Discovery Park in Colchester. The site may have been or included a Roman military supply base that was in use until ca. A.D. 120. The site included several structures, a ditched enclosure, and a possible villa.

name: Great Tey
description: A Romano-British villa complex identified by ploughing in the 1950s.

name: Villa of Manius Antoninus
description: The Villa of Manius Antoninus at Nicopolis.

name: La Chapelle-Vaupelteigne
description: Gallo-Roman villas located in the valley of the Serein, a tributary of the Yonne river, dating from the first to the fourth centuries AD.

name: Port-Sainte-Marie
description: A Gallo-Roman villa located on the right bank of the Garonne river.

name: Villa di Teodorico
description: A palatial Roman villa and bath complex that has been connected with Theodoric the Great, with twentieth century interpretations labeling it as Theodoric's "hunting lodge". More recent excavations have uncovered evidence for earlier building phases, dating from the first century B.C. through the third century A.D.

name: Villa di Fralana
description: The remains of the so-called villa of Fralana belong to the villa rustica type. Mosaic floors have been discovered at the site, in addition to a productive area that may have included a Torcularium.

name: Terme di Tito
description: The so-called "Terme di Tito" is a terraced Roman villa site located in the modern comune of Castel Sant'Angelo near Lago Paterno.

name: Casa Matta at Kaulonia
description: The so-called "Casa Matta" at Kaulonia is an elite domestic building of the Hellenistic period that includes mosaics in the remains of its bathing rooms.

name: House of the outdoor triclinium
description: A Roman domus in Pompeii facing on to the Via Nocera.

name: House of the Tragic Poet
description: The so-called "House of the Tragic Poet" (VI, 8, 3, 5) was built in the first century BC and has the plan of a classic "Samnite" house. The house was excavated in 1824. It lies at the corner of the Via delle Terme and the Via della Fullonica, across from the Forum Baths.

name: House of Caecilius Jucundus
description: The House of Lucius Caecilius Jucundus in Pompeii takes its name from one of its latter-day owners, the banker Lucius Caecilius Iucundus who may have perished in the earthquake of A.D. 62.

name: Villa romana della Standa a Fiano Romano
description: A Roman villa located along the Via Tiberina in present-day Fiano Romano, the footprint of which is preserved within a commercial complex.

name: House of Iphigeneia
description: The House of Pinarius Cerialis and Cassia or Casa dell’orefice or Casa di Ifigenia (III, 4, b).

props.ancientName: House of Pinarius Cerialis and Cassia

name: House of Obellius Firmus
description: The House of M. Obellius Firmus lies south of the Via di Nola and is also known as the House of the Count of Turin. Excavated in 1910, the house's name is derived from electoral graffiti found on the site. (IX, 14, 2, 4, a, b)

name: House of the Prince of Naples
description: An atrium house with an austere facade located on the west side of the Vicolo dei Vettii (VI 15, 7-8).

props.ancientName: House of the Prince of Naples

name: House of Publius Casca Longus
description: The House of Publius Casca Longus (I, 6, 11) is located on the Via dell'Abbondanza and is also known as the Casa dei Quadretti Teatrali.

name: Monte Urralidi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 48 A3 Monte Urralidi

name: Pauli Putzu
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 48 A3 Pauli Putzu

name: Ploaghe
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 48 A2 Ploaghe

name: Quartu S. Elena
description: Quartu Sant'Elena is located along the line of the Roman road at a distance of four miles from Caralis.

name: S. Angiarxia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 48 A3 S. Angiarxia

name: S. Lussurgiu
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 48 A2 S. Lussurgiu

name: S. Ungroni
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 48 A3 S. Ungroni

name: Sant'Imbenia Roman Villa
description: A maritime villa of Roman imperial date, located on the northwest shore of the Porto Conte bay in western Sardinia. Use of the site for various purposes continued into the 8th century of the modern era.

name: Siligo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 48 A2 Siligo

name: Terr'e Frucca
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 48 A3 Terr'e Frucca

name: Ussana
description: A Roman bath structure of the fourth century A.D. whose remains were reused to construct the Medieval church of San Lorenzo.

name: San Cromazio Roman Villa
description: Remains of a late Roman villa with mosaics have been excavated at a site named San Cromazio in the municipal territory of modern Villaspeciosa on the island of Sardinia.

name: Veranes
description: A Roman villa.

name: Roman villa at Tourmont
description: Remains of a large Roman villa and site of a significant coin hoard of the third century A.D.

name: Devia (San Nicandro Garganico)
description: A casale first attested in 1032, Devia is located on Monte Devio (also referred to as Monte d'Elio). This mountain is situated between the coastal lakes of Lesina and Varano, in the northern Gargano.

name: Sant'Annea
description: At Sant'Annèa are the remains of an extensive Roman villa located near the port of Gargano, about 3 km south of Lake Lesina. The villa probably originates in the late republican period and flourishes in the third and fourth centuries CE.

name: Villa Cuomo
description: The so-called Villa Cuomo in the modern Italian comune of Sant'Antonio Abate in greater metropolitan Naples is a Roman villa rustica of the first century CE.

name: Santa Marina (Villa)
description: A large seaside villa on two terraces overlooking the modern Santa Marina bay near Varbiga, Croatia. The villa lies on the northern coast of a headland; the major amphora workshop at Loron occupies the southern coastal site.

name: Roman villa of Capo di Massa
description: A villa at the Capo di Massa that has frequently been associated with Pollius Felix, a magistrate from Puteoli.

name: House of Messius Ampliatus
description: A Pompeian domus excavated in 1916, 1918, and 1921. (II.2.4).

name: Rijswijk
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 10 A4 Rijswijk

name: Marsicovetere Roman villa
description: A Roman villa rustica belonging to the senatorial family of the Bruttii Praesentes that has been connected with Bruttia Crispina, the wife of Commodus. The villa was inhabited from the second century B.C. to the seventh century A.D. The villa's archaeological remains are found in the locality of Barricelle in the territory of the community of Marsicovetere in the province of Potenza.

name: Fórnea Roman villa
description: An imperial villa, occupied between the second and fourth centuries AD.

name: Jemelle
description: A large Gallo-Roman villa located in what is now Belgium.

name: Domus Romana
description: West corner of the museum built on the site of a Roman Villa. Time periods following the Barrington Atlas (BAtlas 47 inset Melita).

name: Massaciuccoli Romana - Padiglione "Il Cantiere"
description: The site of a Roman imperial-period villa in the modern Italian comune of Massaciuccoli Romana.

name: Radwell Roman villa
description: A Roman villa site in Hertfordshire, England, Radwell Roman villa is located 500 meters west of Ermine Street and some 4 km north of the small Roman town at Baldock.

name: Roman villa, via Michelangelo Tilli
description: The remains of the Roman villa located in via Michelangelo Tilli are located close to the Aniene. The late republican villa remained in use until the fourth century.

name: Kaisersteinbruch
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 13 B4 Kaisersteinbruch

name: Parndorf
description: Parndorf was the site of a Roman estate covering some 12 ha. The villa was situated near the Roman road leading from Savaria-Scarbantia to Carnuntum. The site was fortified by means of an enclosure wall ca. 1.5 km long.

name: Camars
description: An Etruscan settlement covering some 17 ha located near the modern village of Gonfienti (Prato).

name: Villa Rufinella
description: Also known as the Villa Tuscolana, this structure was built in the 16th century on the site of a terraced Roman villa. The site occupies one of the most commanding positions on the slope above what is now Frascati, placing it in the environs of ancient Tusculum.

name: Römische Villa
description: A 'villa rustica' located to the west of Höflein, Austria, on the Danube frontier.

name: Great Lemhill Farm Roman villa
description: A Roman villa north-east of Great Lemhill Farm rediscovered in 1937. It remains mostly unexcavated.

name: Little Milton, Ditchend Villa
description: Remains of a Romano-British corridor villa at Little Milton, some 8 miles south-east of Oxford, observed via cropmarks.

name: Abicada
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 B4 Abicada

description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 A3 Alto do Cidreira

name: Casa di Trittolemo
description: The so-called Casa di Trittolemo at Pompeii was excavated in 1859 and again in 1871.

name: House of Thiasus
description: A domus at Pompeii located on Via Nola in insula 10 of Regio IX. Partial excavation at the site occurred in 1888; scientific excavations resumed in 2023. Epigraphic evidence suggests that one of the domus' owners was connected to the duovir Aulus Rustius Verus. In February 2025, a mid-first-century BCE room with a painted Dionysiac cycle was discovered.

name: Domus di Faustus
description: The so-called House of Faustus at Pompeii was excavated in 1844 and 1875. It is linked to V.1.24 and VI.1.26.

name: ville d’Auxerre
description: Located 3 km south of the town of Auxerre, France, on the right bank of the Yonne River, archaeologists excavated the remains of an extensive Gallo-Roman villa covering an area of approximately 1.6 hectares. The villa site includes a bath complex. The site has been noted for its archaeological potential since the nineteenth century. While some excavations took place in the 1960s, the open-area excavation of the villa was announced in 2025.

name: Villa of Agrippa Postumus
description: The Villa of Agrippa Postumus was discovered at Boscotrecase in 1903 on land owned by Cavaliere Ernesto Santini. Works for the Circumvesuviana railroad line brought the site to light. Excavations supervised by Matteo Della Corte were carried out from 1903 to 1905. In 1906, however, a new eruption of Mount Vesuvius reburied the site. Della Corte published a plan of the excavated portion of the villa in the Notizie degli scavi di antichità.

name: Villa romana del Torchio
description: A Roman "villa rustica" with an olive-oil press (torcularium, hence "Torchio") was discovered in 2006.

name: Villa El Soldán (The Sultan)
description: A Roman villa 300 meters east northeast of the eastern tip of Laguna Cernea, north of the modern village Santa Colomba de Somoza. Constructed under Tiberius, it was likely the residence of the official in charge of gold extraction.

name: Casa del Obispo
description: The archaeological site of Casa del Obispo has evidence for multiple urban phases, ranging from the eighth century B.C. to the eighteenth century.

name: Roman villa at Artena
description: A Roman villa built atop the terraced platform of the so-called "Civita di Artena".

name: Bocca di Magra Roman villa
description: A villa maritima of the first century B.C.

props.ancientName: Villa Romana di Bocca di Magra

name: Roman villa of Cortijo Robledo
description: A Roman villa rustica dated between the first and fifth centuries. The site occupies a low hill located to the south of Cerro de Robledo, near the Roman settlement of Aratispi. Archaeological fieldwork has demonstrated that the site was involved in the production of olive oil until the later Roman period.

name: Combley Roman villa
description: The site of a Romano-British villa of the winged corridor type, probably late 3rd century CE in date.

name: Lodge Farm Roman villa
description: Site of a Romano-British villa with a substantial bathhouse near North Warnborough (Hampshire), likely occupied from mid-3rd to late-4th centuries CE.

name: Casa di Pane
description: A domus in Pompeii excavated in 1818. It is linked to VIII.3.28 and VIII.3.30.

name: Brislington Roman villa
description: Site of a late third or fourth century winged corridor-type villa, first excavated in 1899.

name: Twyford
description: Site of Romano-British courtyard villa and bath-house, dating from Flavian-Trajanic period to late 4th/early 5th centuries.

name: House of the Centenary
description: A Pompeian domus re-discovered in 1879, the so-called House of the Centenary is among the largest domus in Pompeii.

name: Mehring
description: Roman villa rustica in Mehring

name: Combe Down
description: Site of a Romano-British villa of courtyard type.

name: Sarresdorf
description: Roman villa rustica

name: Omega House
description: Omega House is the modern name for a large house on the north slope of the Areopagos hill in Athens. Early traces go back to the 4th century BC, but the surviving structure was built in the 4th and 5th centuries AD. It appears to have been a philosophical school until the 6th century. It was destroyed in the Slavic Sack of 582 AD.

name: House of Simon the Shoemaker
description: A house at the southwestern corner of the Athenian Agora, built ca. 500 BC, destroyed during the Persian Sack in 480 BC, rebuilt in the 470s and demolished in the late fourth century BC. Finds suggest that in the third quarter of the fifth century BC it belonged to a shoemaker named Simon, perhaps to be identified with the interlocutor of Sokrates known from literary sources.

name: Roman villa at Santo André de Almoçageme
description: A Roman villa located in Santo André de Almoçageme, Sintra, Portugal, and occupied from the second to the sixth centuries AD.

name: Villa of Horace
description: The archaeological remains of a villa near Licenza, Italy, that are traditionally connected with the Latin poet Horace.

name: Domus of the figurative emblem
description: The so-called "Domus of the figurative emblem" is a well-appointed domus at Privernum.

name: Pieve a Bozzone
description: The ruins of an ancient Roman villa were discovered in the Italian comune of Pieve a Bozzone (Siena) in 2023.

name: Saint-Saphorin
description: A Roman villa located near the shores of Lake Geneva.

name: Mané-Vechen, Plouhinec
description: A Gallo-Roman villa excavated in 1970-1971. A possible fanum was identified 50 meters to the northwest of the villa.

name: House of the Ship Europa
description: The so-called "House of the Ship Europa" is a large house in the city of Pompeii (I,15,3) that takes its conventional, archaeological name from a large graffito of ship bearing the legend 'Europa'.

name: House of Lucius Cornelius Diadumenus
description: The House of Lucius Cornelius Diadumenus (VII, 12, 26) is a small domus noted for its wall paintings.

name: Villa della Pisanella
description: A Roman villa rustica at Boscoreale that was first discovered in 1868 by Modestino Pulzella. The villa is best known as the findspot of the so-called "Boscoreale Treasure". Following excavation, the remains of the villa were reinterred.

name: Hoogeloon Roman villa
description: Remains of a Roman villa were identified to the east of the Dutch village of Hoogeloon in the commune of Bladel, in North Brabant.

name: Domvs Romana
description: A ruined Roman townhouse located on the boundary between Mdina and Rabat, Malta that was discovered in 1881. It dates to the first century B.C.

name: House A VII 4
description: Olynthian "type house" for the orthogonally planned urban area on the North Hill

description: Modern Langon is a commune located in the Ille-et-Vilaine department of Brittany in northwestern France. Various archaeological remains from the Roman period survive there, including paved roads, the remains of a villa, and the so-called Chapelle Sainte-Agathe.

name: Unnamed Gallo-Roman villa (Montlauzun)
description: Majonelle, Cm. Montlauzun, France, was the site of a long-lived villa of substantial size. According to M. Labrousse writing in PECS, the presence of a "Byzantine coin of Phokis" denotes a very late occupation.

name: Unnamed villa
description: A Roman villa located near Touron, Cm. Monségur.

name: Barrington Atlas feature labeled 113
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 F4 no. 113 (St-Martial-d’Artenset)

name: Mageroy Gallo-Roman villa
description: The archaeological site of a well preserved Gallo-Roman villa located at Mageroy in Habay-la-Vieille, Arlon.

name: Villa of Good Fortune
description: A well-appointed "villa" at Olynthus excavated in 1934, the so-called "Villa of Good Fortune" was considered one of the two "type" houses for the ancient site. It was located in the southeastern part of the city in the so-called "villa quarter". Its remains are no longer visible.

name: Passo Lombardo
description: A villa rustica in Rome's suburbium with evidence for domestic production in the form of vats made from peperino tuff.

name: Villa romana delle Grotte
description: A villa dating to the final quarter of the first century BC located on a headland overlooking Portoferraio on the island of Elba.

name: Villa Area 5-7 a Tor Vergata
description: An imperial villa located at Tor Vergata in the suburbium of Rome.

name: Domus delle bestie ferite
description: The Domus delle Bestie Ferite (“House of the Wounded Animals”) in Aquileia is an elite townhouse with at least two architectural phases: the first belonging to the Augustan period and the second to Late Antiquity. The house is well-appointed with elaborate mosaic pavements.

name: Canneto sul Trigno
description: The remains of a Roman "Villa rustica" located adjacent to the Sanctuary of the Madonna di Canneto.

name: House of the Wooden Shrine
description: The so-called House of the Wooden Shrine may have been owned by one Lucius Autronius Euthymius. Its remains include a notable wooden shrine.

name: House of the Gem
description: The so-called House of the Gem at Herculaneum was once linked to the House of the Relief of Telephus when Marcus Nonius Balbus owned the complex. After the Augustan period, the two domus were separated, and the House of the Gem was restored following the earthquake of A.D. 62.

name: Bax Farm, Teynham
description: A Romano-British villa with an octagonal bathhouse was documented at Bax Farm.

name: Coue d'Auzenat
description: A Gallo-Roman villa.

props.ancientName: Villa gallo-romaine de la Coue d'Auzenat

name: House of the Moralist
description: The so-called House of the Moralist, also known as the House of M. Epidius Hymenaeus, is located on the Via dell'Abbondanza. The house was formed from two connecting properties that belonged to two related wine-merchants, T. Arrius Polites and M. Epidius Hymenaeus ( III, 4, 2-3).

name: Unnamed villa
description: An unnamed villa of Tripolitana

name: Unnamed villa
description: An unnamed villa of Tripolitana

name: Unnamed villa
description: An unnamed villa of Tripolitana

name: Villa of Furius Octavianus
description: A Roman villa associated with a Caius Furius Octavianus who was a member of the Senatorial order during the Severan period. The villa was identified along the via Aurelia in the locality of Massimina - Casal Lumbroso. Excavations were carried out between 2011 and 2017.

name: Los Casarejos, San Martín de Losa
description: A Roman villa.

name: House of Minucius
description: The so-called House of Minucius is located in Regio I of the city of Pompeii at the intersection of Vicolo del Menandro and Vicolo del Citarista. It is noted for two plastered benches flanking the fauces as well as painted electoral graffiti.

name: Cala Maestra
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 41 E5 Cala Maestra

name: Domitiana
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 41 E5 Domitiana

name: Massaciuccoli
description: The Roman-period remains at Massaciuccoli, including luxury villas, baths, and productive areas, are connected with the influential Venulei family. Perhaps of Etruscan origins, the Venulei are also to be linked with Pisa.

name: La Pieve
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 41 D3 La Pieve

name: Puntone Vecchio
description: A Roman villa that was likely in use from the first century BC until the third century AD.

name: S. Liberata
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 41 E5 S. Liberata

name: S. Vincenzino
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 41 C3 S. Vincenzino

name: Alviano Scalo
description: Occupation of the Roman villa at Alviano Scalo begins in the first century BCE and continues until the fourth century CE.

name: Colle Plinio
description: A hilltop villa near Tifernum Tiberinum commonly associated with one of the villas of Pliny the Younger.

name: Grotte di S. Nicola
description: The so-called Grotte di S. Nicola, the remains of the platform and cryptoporticus of a late republican Roman villa.

name: Grotte di Torri
description: Grotte di Torri is located on the left bank of the Tiber river in the modern comune of Montopoli di Sabina. It has been inhabited since pre-Roman times.

name: Monte Calvo
description: The remains of a Roman villa that belonged to the family of the Bruttii Praesentes were found in the locality known as 'Madonna dei Colori' near the ancient road station Vicus Novus/Ad Novas, modern Osteria Nuova, some 53 km northeast of Rome.

name: Ossaia
description: Site of a Late Republican and Imperial Roman villa (first to fifth centuries A.D.)

name: Ponte Capo d’Acqua
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 D4 Ponte Capo d’Acqua

name: S. Valentino
description: Remains of at least two Roman villas have been identified in the frazione of San Valentino, part of the larger comune of Poggio Mirteto. One is the so-called Villa dei Casoni, while this site is sometimes known as the Bagni di Lucilla. This villa was the findspot in 1801 of a mosaic pavement now in the Chiaramonti museums of the Vatican.

name: Settefinestre
description: A Late Republican Roman slave-run villa owned by the senatorial family of the Volusii.

name: Talamone
description: Located along the road from Talamone to Cannelle are the archaeological remains of one of the largest maritime villas known in the Albegna valley. Referred to colloquially as Villa le Terme at Santa Francesca di Talamone, the remains of this site can be divided into four sectors: the cistern, in a locality referred to as "Le Tombe", the villa platform (basis villae) and core of the villa referred to as "Le Terme", the cistern in an area known as "Santa Francesca", and the remains of poorly preserved outbuildings.

props.ancientName: Santa Francesca di Talamone - Villa le Terme

name: Traianus?
description: A Roman villa that is typically associated with the emperor Trajan.

name: Vicarello
description: A large, Roman imperial estate covering ca. 2,000 ha on the shores of Lake Bracciano. The name may derive from Vicus Aurelius, suggesting a connection with Marcus Aurelius.

name: Volusii
description: A villa associated with the Volusii Saturnini built ca. 50 B.C.

name: Ad Duas Lauros
description: The toponym 'Ad Duas Lauros' refers to a large imperial property at the third mile of the Via Labicana and today comprising the archaeological park of Centocelle. The site is mentioned in the Liber Pontificalis as that of a large imperial villa in which Helena, mother of Constantine I, lived. Archaeological work conducted in the late 1990s identified the remains of substantial Republican-period villas in the same area, with evidence of both elite dwellings and viticulture. The villas identified are the Villa della Piscina and the Villa Ad Duas Lauros.

name: Adriano
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 Adriano

name: Albuccione
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Albuccione

name: Villa degli Antonini
description: The so-called 'Villa of the Antonines', located near Genzano di Roma in the Alban Hills, lies just south of the Via Appia between the road's 18th and 19th milestones.

name: Arcinazzo
description: An imperial villa of the Trajanic and Hadrianic periods located on the Altipiani di Arcinazzo.

name: Il Barco
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Il Barco

name: Bel Poggio
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Bel Poggio

name: Bruto e Pisoni
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 Bruto e Pisoni

name: Cappellette
description: The site of a villa located between Frascati and Monte Porzio Catone. It is associated with Matidia Augusta on account of an inscribed fistula discovered there in 1888.

name: Casale del Cavaliere
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Casale del Cavaliere

name: Casale di Gregna
description: A villa at the seventh mile of the Via Latina.

name: Casale Martellona
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Casale Martellona

name: Casale Morena
description: A villa located at the eighth mile of the ancient Via Latina. The villa was first built in the first century B.C. and continued in use until the fourth century. During its life span there were several phases of architectural modification and expansion.

name: Casali Vitriano
description: T. Ashby notes the presence of a villa platform to the west of the "upper Casale Vitriano". This platform seems to be a terrace at least 34 m in length.

name: Case Nuove 2
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Case Nuove 2

name: Case Rosse
description: Ashby describes a rich archaeological landscape that once included the remains of Roman-period tombs and villas, the latter appointed with mosaics. The modern toponym "Case Rosse" is said to derive from a well-preserved Roman tomb executed in brick that was purposefully destroyed in 1824.

name: Cassio
description: A villa in Tibur that has been incorrectly linked to Gaius Cassius Longinus. In the eighteenth century, the site produced a cache of Egyptianizing sculpture that likely dates to the reign of Hadrian and is now held in the Vatican Museums.

name: Centroni
description: The Villa dei Centroni, located on the modern via Anagnina, lay at the ninth mile of the ancient via Latina. It dates from the first century B.C. onward.

name: Cinecittà
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Cinecittà

name: Civitana
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D3 Civitana

name: Clodio
description: Cicero describes the villa of Publius Clodius Pulcher as being located at the thirteenth mile of the Via Appia near Bovillae.

name: Colle Medico
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D3 Colle Medico

name: Colle Nocello
description: A plaform villa at Colle Nocello.

name: Colle Rosa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 Colle Rosa

name: Colle Tasso
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Colle Tasso

name: Colle Vitriano
description: Colle Vitriano is the site of a Roman villa supported on two artificial terraces that utilize megalithic walling in the polygonal masonry technique as well as caementicium.

name: Corzano
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Corzano

name: Forte Tiburtina
description: At Forte Tiburtina the remains of a Roman villa located on the northern edge of the Parco della Serenissima in the eastern suburbium of the city of Rome.

name: Fosso della Crescenza
description: John Ward-Perkins identified the archaeological site at Fosso della Crescenza during the South Etruria Survey. Excavations of a Iulio-Claudian mausoleum were undertaken in 1962. Additional excavation revealed portions of the Via Veientana. Post-excavation work did not occur until the 1980s, by which time Fentress 1983 notes that the archaeological landscape had been largely eradicated by the burgeoning growth of suburban Rome.

name: Frascati
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Frascati

name: Villa Gordiani
description: A third century A.D. villa complex along the Via Praenestina connected with Gordian I, Gordian II, and Gordian III.

name: Grotta del Mortale
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 Grotta del Mortale

name: Grotta Papale
description: The site of a Roman villa situated on two terraces. The modern toponym "Grotta Papale" derives from a partially subterranean apsidal nymphaeum. The villa was extensive, covering some 23,000 square meters and has both a late republican and an imperial phase.

name: Grottaferrata
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Grottaferrata

name: Inviolata
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Inviolata

name: Ladispoli
description: Ladispoli is a modern toponym; it is a comune of the Metropolitan City of Rome and is situated 35 kilometers (22 miles) west of Rome, on the Mediterranean Sea coast. In terms of the ancient landscape, present-day Ladispoli lies in the environs of ancient Alsium. Based on archaeological remains, Ladispoli was a popular locus for coastal villas.

name: Laghetto di Colonna
description: Based on T. Ashby (1902), the compilers of the Barrington Atlas include a Roman villa site identified as "Laghetto di Colonna". Ashby discusses this toponym in relation to the discussion about the location of Lacus Regillus; he posits that Laghetto di Colonna and Lacus Regillus are, in fact, different places. Ashby describes Laghetto di Colonna as the site of extensive quarry works in the early twentieth century, and that the remains of an extensive Roman villa occupy the cliffs to the north and east of the basin. Nearby is another villa that may have been owned by one Q. Pompeius Falco, as discussed by Rodolfo Lanciani.

name: Lolli Paolini
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 Lolli Paolini

name: Lucio Vero
description: A Roman villa located at Acquatraversa on the Via Cassia that is frequently attributed to Lucius Verus.

name: Lucullo
description: Plutarch describes a villa owned by Lucullus near Tusculum.

name: Marco Pedonio
description: The villa of Marcus Pedonius.

name: Marina di S. Nicola
description: Marina di S. Nicola is located close to Alsium.

name: Mondragone
description: A Renaissance villa (Villa Mondragone) was built atop the platform of a Roman villa dating to the first century B.C.

name: Montedoro
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Montedoro

name: Munazio Planco
description: Munazio Planco, the so-called Villa of Munatius Plancus and villa on Via delle Piagge.

name: Naumachia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 B2 Naumachia

name: Villa of the Octavii
description: The archaeological remains of a Roman villa on the hill of San Cesareo are speculatively identified as belonging to the gens Octavia, according to Della Portella 2004 op. cit.

name: Opimiano
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Opimiano

name: Ospedale di S. Camillo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 B2 Ospedale di S. Camillo

name: Pianelle
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 Pianelle

name: Plinio
description: The so-called villa of Pliny the Younger, also known as the Villa della Palombara. The villa's first phases date to the Iulio-Claudian period. It continues in use, and continues to undergo modifications, through the second century A.D.

name: Ponte di Tuscolo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Ponte di Tuscolo

name: Prati Fiscali
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Prati Fiscali

name: Prima Porta
description: Prima Porta, located to the north of Rome, allegedly derives its modern name from the remains of a Roman aqueduct whose presence signaled to travelers on the Via Flaminia that they had reached Rome. The site is best known for an Imperial villa that belonged to the family of Livia Drusilla.

name: Quadraro
description: A Roman villa near the line of the Aqua Claudia located in the modern Via del Quadraro. The villa is dated to the first century B.C. or first century A.D. Following remodelling in the third century, the villa's occupation ends in the fourth century.

name: Quintilii
description: A suburban villa built by Sextus Quintilius Maximus and Sextus Quintilius Condianus (consuls in A.D. 151).

name: Quintilio Varo
description: A villa located near the sanctuary of Madonna di Quintiliolo (Tivoli) that is traditionally associated with Quintilius Varus. The remains of the basis villae may date as early as the third century B.C.

name: S. Antonio
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 S. Antonio

name: S. Carlo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 S. Carlo

name: S. Cesareo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 S. Cesareo

name: S. Martino
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 S. Martino

name: Scalzacane
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 Scalzacane

name: Sette Bassi
description: A large, suburban villa complex on the Via Tuscolana. The villa was in use from the reign of Antoninus Pius until the fourth century A.D.

name: Settebagni
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Settebagni

name: Tor de’ Cenci
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 B2 Tor de’ Cenci

name: Tor Marancia
description: The modern toponym Tor Marancia is claimed to derive from the Praedium Amaranthianus, a villa belonging to the second-century A.D. freedman Amaranthus.

name: Tor Paterno
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 B3 Tor Paterno

name: Torre Caldara
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C4 Torre Caldara

name: Torre S. Angelo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 D2 Torre S. Angelo

name: Valerio Messala Corvino
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 C2 Valerio Messala Corvino

name: House of Meleager
description: The so-called "House of Meleager" (VI.9.2) is a domus in Pompeii of the "Samnite" type that was excavated first in 1829, then again in 1836 and 1966. The interior boasts rich painted decoration corresponding to Mau's first, third, and fourth styles.

name: Buxio
description: Buxio is one of several villas mentioned in a list of "donatio ecclesiae" in the "territorio Carducino" in the Life of Saint Didier, who was bishop of Cahors in the 7th century CE. BAtlas places it with a hollow symbol and a question mark on map 25, offering "Le Buz, Vabre?" in the directory for modern location. Modern Vabre is a French commune in Occitania on the Gijou river. CAG reports that the remains of a villa had been reported in the 1940s in Vabre, but without further specificity.

name: Domus di Vigna Guidi
description: A well-appointed domus of the mid-second century CE that was consumed by the construction works to create the Thermae Antoninianae, resulting in its being buried to a depth of some 10 m in construction fill. Heritage officials describe the house as a sort of 'condominium', comparing it to the contemporary insulae of Ostia.

name: Orton Longueville

name: Villa delle Terme degli Stucchi Dipinti

name: Villa of Manlius Vopiscus
description: A villa site near Tivoli that has long been associated Manlius Vopiscus, who Statius mentions. Excavations began in 1825. In 1835 Pope Gregory XVI began construction of his eponymous Villa Gregoriana on the grounds of the Roman villa.

name: Unnamed Roman villa (Ladispoli)
description: The ruins of a well-appointed Roman Imperial villa are now to be seen immured in the esplanade of one of the beachfronts of Ladispoli, Italy. The visible remains include a portion of a peristyle courtyard with an elaborate mosaic pavement.

name: Domus, M. Tullius Cicero
description: The house of the Roman statesman and orator M. Tullius Cicero was located on the north-eastern corner of the Palatine Hill in Rome.
